title: Directional travel coherence phase
summary: Thirty-fourth structured packet for Eco Explorer. After the season-capstone wave, lane 1 should run a focused directional travel coherence pass so corridor entries, exits, world-map returns, and travel-facing cues feel spatially believable and less disorienting without rewriting the hybrid map-plus-corridor model. The scout audit narrows the first main pass to stable same-biome map-return anchoring, tiny target-aware travel cues, and direction-first world-map footer copy. The follow-up scout handoff narrows the final pass to shared menu and post map-exit framing plus one tiny world-map origin reminder.

execution_order.goal: Audit the current travel chain for directional incoherence.
execution_order.why_now: The current hybrid travel model works, but orientation is still one of the biggest remaining player-facing clarity issues.

execution_order.goal: Implement the first directional travel coherence pass.
execution_order.why_now: This is the main systems polish step that makes the world feel more connected.

execution_order.goal: Review the directional travel coherence pass.
execution_order.why_now: The pass only succeeds if it reduces disorientation without becoming over-guided.
There will likely be a small number of stubborn confusion points worth a second, lighter pass.

execution_order.goal: Implement the directional follow-up pass: shared map-exit framing plus a tiny origin reminder.
execution_order.why_now: A small follow-up should finish the travel-coherence lane more cleanly than one oversized first pass.

execution_order.goal: Review the completed directional travel phase.
execution_order.why_now: This is the gate for whether travel now supports the ecosystem-gradient fantasy clearly enough.

guardrails: Do not replace the hybrid map-plus-corridor model.
Do not add a large new HUD or navigation panel.
Prefer spatial framing, anchors, and subtle cues over tutorial text.
Keep the pass in lane 1 and centered on coherence, not on broader content expansion.
Preserve the cozy exploratory tone while reducing disorientation.
acceptance_focus: Travel feels less spatially backward or disorienting.
Map returns, corridor entries, and exit framing feel more coherent.
The world reads more like one connected gradient.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-1.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-directional-travel-coherence-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as a lane-1 systems polish wave. Keep the hybrid map-plus-corridor model, but make biome-to-biome travel feel directionally coherent and less disorienting through better framing, anchors, and subtle travel cues.
